forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS36QGEVVP064EKW0JDGD94B
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8980'
+project: command-service
+title: 'Builtin plugin: file/board/window commands (port file.yaml)'
+---
+## What
+
+Port `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/file.yaml` (5 commands: `file.switchBoard`, `file.closeBoard`, `file.newBoard`, `file.openBoard`, `window.new`) to a builtin TypeScript plugin.
+
+Files:
+- `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts` ŌĆö registers all 5
+
+These commands cross into the host shell (board file management, window creation), so several will route through the forthcoming `window` MCP server (defined in a later task) rather than the kanban server. That's fine ŌĆö at registration time the plugin just declares the command; the callback target is decided at call time.
+
+Sequencing note: this plugin can register the commands before the `window` MCP server exists; the `execute` callbacks will fail at runtime if `window` isn't registered yet, but that's caught by the frontend e2e tests, not by the plugin-load tests. Ordering with the `window` server task is loose ŌĆö the cut-over task gates the final user-visible behavior.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `builtin/plugins/file-commands/` discoverable
+- [ ] All 5 commands appear in `list command` with original metadata
+- [ ] `execute` for each, when its downstream MCP server is registered, produces the same effect as today's YAML version
+- [ ] Metadata (keys, scope, params, menu placement) matches the YAML baseline exactly
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_file_commands_e2e.rs` ŌĆö load plugin; assert all 5 registered with metadata fidelity; execute `file.newBoard` (which only touches the store, no window needed) and assert the new board file appears
+- [ ] Metadata-fidelity tests for each command
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration builtin_file_commands_e2e` passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`
\ No newline at end of file

+title: 'Builtin plugin: file/board commands (port file.yaml)'
 ---
 ## What

-Port `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/file.yaml` (5 commands: `file.switchBoard`, `file.closeBoard`, `file.newBoard`, `file.openBoard`, `window.new`) to a builtin TypeScript plugin.
+Port `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/file.yaml` (4 commands: `file.switchBoard`, `file.closeBoard`, `file.newBoard`, `file.openBoard`) to a builtin TypeScript plugin.
+
+(NOTE: `window.new` lives in `ui.yaml`, not `file.yaml` ŌĆö it is handled by the ui-commands plugin task.)

 Files:
-- `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts` ŌĆö registers all 5
+- `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts` ŌĆö registers all 4

-These commands cross into the host shell (board file management, window creation), so several will route through the forthcoming `window` MCP server (defined in a later task) rather than the kanban server. That's fine ŌĆö at registration time the plugin just declares the command; the callback target is decided at call time.
+These commands cross into the host shell (board file management), so several will route through the `window` MCP server (defined in a later task) for OS file dialogs, plus the kanban server for new-board creation. That's fine ŌĆö at registration time the plugin just declares the command; the callback target is decided at call time.

 Sequencing note: this plugin can register the commands before the `window` MCP server exists; the `execute` callbacks will fail at runtime if `window` isn't registered yet, but that's caught by the frontend e2e tests, not by the plugin-load tests. Ordering with the `window` server task is loose ŌĆö the cut-over task gates the final user-visible behavior.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `builtin/plugins/file-commands/` discoverable
-- [ ] All 5 commands appear in `list command` with original metadata
+- [ ] All 4 commands appear in `list command` with original metadata
 - [ ] `execute` for each, when its downstream MCP server is registered, produces the same effect as today's YAML version
 - [ ] Metadata (keys, scope, params, menu placement) matches the YAML baseline exactly

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_file_commands_e2e.rs` ŌĆö load plugin; assert all 5 registered with metadata fidelity; execute `file.newBoard` (which only touches the store, no window needed) and assert the new board file appears
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_file_commands_e2e.rs` ŌĆö load plugin; assert all 4 registered with metadata fidelity; execute `file.newBoard` (which only touches the store) and assert the new board file appears
 - [ ] Metadata-fidelity tests for each command
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration builtin_file_commands_e2e` passes

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@
 (NOTE: `window.new` lives in `ui.yaml`, not `file.yaml` ŌĆö it is handled by the ui-commands plugin task.)

 Files:
-- `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts` ŌĆö registers all 4
+- `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts` ŌĆö entry; `load()` calls `ensureServices(this, ["commands", "window"])` then `registerCommands(this, [...])` with all 4 commands. The `window` service is needed for `file.openBoard` (OS file dialog).

 These commands cross into the host shell (board file management), so several will route through the `window` MCP server (defined in a later task) for OS file dialogs, plus the kanban server for new-board creation. That's fine ŌĆö at registration time the plugin just declares the command; the callback target is decided at call time.

@@ -26,6 +26,7 @@
 - [ ] All 4 commands appear in `list command` with original metadata
 - [ ] `execute` for each, when its downstream MCP server is registered, produces the same effect as today's YAML version
 - [ ] Metadata (keys, scope, params, menu placement) matches the YAML baseline exactly
+- [ ] `load()` calls `ensureServices` before `registerCommands` (the convention)

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_file_commands_e2e.rs` ŌĆö load plugin; assert all 4 registered with metadata fidelity; execute `file.newBoard` (which only touches the store) and assert the new board file appears
